{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which path should we take to “make this stronger”? A) Micro-level writing polish and tone only (tighten sentences, improve clarity/concision, sharpen wording without changing claims, structure, or scope), or B) Macro-level argument strengthening (reframe the core claim and contribution, tighten the logic and evidence-claim links, and, if needed, modestly reorganize sections and calibrate claim strength without adding new experiments)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely involves sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level to emphasize what PolySolve offers.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger version should have a crisper argument chain from method construction to the disagreement metric to conclusions about numerical sensitivity.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about uncertainty flag rates and correlation with conditioning should be tightly aligned with the reported results to support stronger statements.",
    "R1.6": "Rhetorical strength often comes from clearer, more concise sentences and improved flow without altering technical content.",
    "RX.1": "Any strengthening should transparently disclose limits and uncertainties (e.g., tau calibration, panel composition) to avoid misleading certainty.",
    "RX.2": "When making the prose more forceful, we must avoid overstating generality, robustness, or causal claims beyond what the reported evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "The work must remain within the scope of strengthening the existing artifact rather than introducing new experiments or unreported claims.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid edits that could encourage unsafe overreliance on the tool in high-stakes settings by overstating verification guarantees.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear traceability to the artifact’s stated methods, metrics, solvers, and problems so readers can verify what was actually done."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which path should we take to “make this stronger”? A) Micro-level writing polish and tone only (tighten sentences, improve clarity/concision, sharpen wording without changing claims, structure, or scope), or B) Macro-level argument strengthening (reframe the core claim and contribution, tighten the logic and evidence-claim links, and, if needed, modestly reorganize sections and calibrate claim strength without adding new experiments)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely involves sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level to emphasize what PolySolve offers.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger version should have a crisper argument chain from method construction to the disagreement metric to conclusions about numerical sensitivity.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about uncertainty flag rates and correlation with conditioning should be tightly aligned with the reported results to support stronger statements.
rationale.R1.6: Rhetorical strength often comes from clearer, more concise sentences and improved flow without altering technical content.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthening should transparently disclose limits and uncertainties (e.g., tau calibration, panel composition) to avoid misleading certainty.
rationale.RX.2: When making the prose more forceful, we must avoid overstating generality, robustness, or causal claims beyond what the reported evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The work must remain within the scope of strengthening the existing artifact rather than introducing new experiments or unreported claims.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid edits that could encourage unsafe overreliance on the tool in high-stakes settings by overstating verification guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear traceability to the artifact’s stated methods, metrics, solvers, and problems so readers can verify what was actually done.
